title: EU AI Act Enters into Force
summary: The European Union's Artificial Intelligence Act — the world's first comprehensive AI regulation — entered into force. The Act establishes a risk-based framework classifying AI systems into unacceptable risk (banned), high risk (heavily regulated), limited risk (transparency requirements), and minimal risk (unregulated). It introduces specific requirements for foundation models and general-purpose AI, including transparency about training data and compute usage. The Act has extraterritorial reach, affecting any AI system deployed to EU users.
impact_description: Established the first comprehensive legal framework for AI worldwide. Set a precedent that other jurisdictions are following or reacting against. Created compliance requirements that affect every major AI company's global strategy.
counterfactual: Without the EU AI Act, AI regulation might remain fragmented and voluntary for years longer. The act serves as a template — and a catalyst — for regulatory efforts worldwide. Its absence would leave AI governance in a more ambiguous state during a period of rapid capability growth.
query_hooks: What is the EU AI Act?
How are AI systems classified by risk?
What are the legal requirements for AI models?
